title: eumachia rostrata
intro: Eumachia rostrata, also known as Mexican buckthorn or buckthorn, is a shrub native to Central America that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Eumachia rostrata include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as having antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Eumachia rostrata is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
